api/v3/handlers/meters.purpose: v3 HTTP handler package for all meter CRUD and query operations. Bridges the generated api/v3 request/response types to meter.ManageService and streaming.Connector via the httptransport.Handler pattern. Also owns the query/ sub-package for translating MeterQueryRequest fields into streaming.QueryParams.

patterns.name: Type-alias triplets per operation
patterns.description: Each operation file declares three type aliases: <Op>Request, <Op>Response, <Op>Handler. Request/Response alias domain input/output types directly; Handler aliases httptransport.Handler or HandlerWithArgs.
patterns.example: type CreateMeterRequest = meter.CreateMeterInput; type CreateMeterResponse = api.Meter; type CreateMeterHandler httptransport.Handler[CreateMeterRequest, CreateMeterResponse]

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs for path-param endpoints
patterns.description: Use NewHandlerWithArgs when the endpoint has a path parameter (meterID string). Use NewHandler only for endpoints without path params (e.g. CreateMeter).

patterns.name: Namespace always resolved in decoder
patterns.description: h.resolveNamespace(ctx) is called in the decoder closure (first argument), never in the operation closure. Errors from resolveNamespace are returned immediately.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return Request{}, err }

patterns.name: apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder on every handler
patterns.description: Every httptransport.NewHandler / NewHandlerWithArgs call appends httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()) in the options block via httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, ...).

patterns.name: Reserved dimension validation in decoder
patterns.description: For Create and Update, call validateDimensionsWithoutReserved(*body.Dimensions) in the decoder before constructing the domain input. This calls query.IsReservedDimension per dimension key.
patterns.example: if body.Dimensions != nil { if err := validateDimensionsWithoutReserved(*body.Dimensions); err != nil { return Request{}, err } }

patterns.name: Query path delegates to query.BuildQueryParams
patterns.description: Both QueryMeter and QueryMeterCSV call query.BuildQueryParams(ctx, m, req.Body, query.NewCustomerResolver(h.customerService)) — never inline the param-building logic in the handler.

patterns.name: Goverter-generated conversion functions initialized in init()
patterns.description: convert.gen.go sets package-level var functions (FromAPICreateMeterRequest, ToAPIMeter, etc.) in init(). Never call these before init runs; never hand-edit convert.gen.go.
patterns.example: var FromAPICreateMeterRequest func(namespace string, req api.CreateMeterRequest) (meter.CreateMeterInput, error)

key_file_guides.role: Defines Handler interface (all 7 method signatures) and handler struct with resolveNamespace, service, streaming, customerService, options. New() constructor injects all dependencies.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add domain service calls directly to handler struct methods; those belong in per-operation files.

key_file_guides.role: Hand-written conversion helpers (ToAPIMeterAggregation, FromAPIMeterAggregation, ToAPIMeterQueryRow, ToAPIMeterQueryResult, FromAPIUpdateMeterRequest) and Goverter annotations that drive convert.gen.go generation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Goverter var declarations (FromAPICreateMeterRequest, ToAPIMeter) must not be called manually — they are wired in init() by convert.gen.go.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by Goverter. DO NOT EDIT. Contains init() that sets all package-level conversion function vars.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any hand-edit will be overwritten by 'go generate ./...'. Re-run make generate after changing convert.go annotations.

key_file_guides.role: Declares ErrCodeReservedDimension ErrorCode constant and ErrReservedDimension ValidationIssue sentinel with HTTP 400 attribute. NewReservedDimensionError adds path and value attrs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All new validation errors in this package must follow the same pattern: package-level sentinel + constructor that calls .WithPathString/.WithAttr.

key_file_guides.role: validateDimensionsWithoutReserved — iterates a map and returns NewReservedDimensionError if any key matches query.IsReservedDimension.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New reserved dimensions must be added in query/dimensions.go (IsReservedDimension), not here.

key_file_guides.role: QueryMeterCSV handler plus queryMeterCSVResult type implementing commonhttp.CSVResponse. Column order: from, to, [subject,] [customer_id, customer_key, customer_name,] <other dims...>, value.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Customer enrichment (key/name) is done post-query via ListCustomers; any new reserved dimension column must be added to both the header and the record-building loop.

key_file_guides.role: Sub-package owning BuildQueryParams, IsReservedDimension, CustomerResolverFunc, filter operator extraction, and ISO 8601 ↔ WindowSize lookup. All query-building logic lives here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not inline query parameter translation in the parent handlers; always delegate to BuildQueryParams.

anti_patterns: Using httptransport.NewHandler instead of NewHandlerWithArgs when the endpoint takes a path parameter (meterID).
Calling h.resolveNamespace in the operation closure instead of the decoder closure.
Adding a new dimension key directly in the meters package instead of updating query.IsReservedDimension in query/dimensions.go.
Hand-editing convert.gen.go — it is overwritten by go generate.
Omitting httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()) from the options block, which breaks domain-error-to-HTTP-status mapping.

decisions.decision: query/ is a separate sub-package rather than inline code in query.go
decisions.rationale: Isolates all MeterQueryRequest → streaming.QueryParams translation (dimension validation, filter extraction, customer resolution, window-size mapping) so handlers stay thin and the logic is independently testable without wiring an HTTP context.

decisions.decision: Conversion functions are Goverter-generated vars (not methods)
decisions.rationale: Goverter generates type-safe mapping code at build time; package-level vars initialized in init() allow the generated code to live in a separate file while being callable from hand-written handlers without any receiver.

Pre-building the base issue (ErrReservedDimension) as a sentinel lets the HTTP error encoder read the embedded HTTP status code attribute, while per-call constructors add field-specific path/value context without duplicating the base message.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new CRUD endpoint (e.g. CloneMeter) following the established handler pattern

api/v3/handlers/plans.purpose: v3 HTTP handler package for all plan CRUD and lifecycle operations (create, get, list, update, delete, publish, archive). Bridges generated api/v3 types to plan.Service and productcatalog domain types. Owns a rich convert.go with hand-written bidirectional transformations for the multi-level Plan → Phase → RateCard → Price hierarchy. The planaddons/ child handles the plan-addons sub-resource.

patterns.name: Type-alias triplets per operation
patterns.description: Each operation file declares <Op>Request, <Op>Response, <Op>Handler. Request/Response alias domain input/output types directly.
patterns.example: type CreatePlanRequest = plan.CreatePlanInput; type CreatePlanResponse = api.BillingPlan; type CreatePlanHandler httptransport.Handler[CreatePlanRequest, CreatePlanResponse]

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs for path-param endpoints; NewHandler for no-param
patterns.description: Endpoints with a planID path param use NewHandlerWithArgs. CreatePlan (no path param) uses NewHandler.

patterns.name: Namespace resolved in decoder, never operation
patterns.description: h.resolveNamespace(ctx) is always the first call in the decoder closure.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return Request{}, err }

patterns.name: apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder on every handler
patterns.description: Every handler options block must include httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()) via httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, ...).

patterns.name: IgnoreNonCriticalIssues=true on create and update
patterns.description: After FromAPICreatePlanRequest / FromAPIUpsertPlanRequest, set req.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues = true before returning from the decoder. This allows plans with non-critical validation issues to be saved.
patterns.example: req, err := FromAPICreatePlanRequest(ns, body); ...; req.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues = true; return req, nil

patterns.name: Nil-check on service response for mutating operations
patterns.description: CreatePlan, UpdatePlan, PublishPlan, ArchivePlan check for p == nil after the service call and return a descriptive error. Get/Delete do not need this check.
patterns.example: if p == nil { return CreatePlanResponse{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to create plan") }

patterns.name: Price type exhaustive switch with unsupported-type guard
patterns.description: ToAPIBillingPrice uses an exhaustive switch on p.Type(). DynamicPriceType and PackagePriceType return models.NewGenericConflictError. ListPlans filters out plans containing unsupported prices via hasUnsupportedV3Price.
patterns.example: case productcatalog.DynamicPriceType: return result, models.NewGenericConflictError(fmt.Errorf("dynamic price is not supported in v3 API"))

key_file_guides.role: Defines Handler interface (7 method signatures) and handler struct with resolveNamespace, service (plan.Service), and options. New() constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: plan.Service is the only domain service injected here — unlike meters, there is no streaming or customer service dependency at this level.

key_file_guides.role: All bidirectional conversion: ToAPIBillingPlan, ToAPIBillingPlanPhase, ToAPIBillingRateCard, ToAPIBillingPrice, ToAPIBillingPriceTiers, ToAPIBillingRateCardTaxConfig, ToAPIBillingRateCardDiscount, ToAPIBillingSpendCommitments, ToAPIProductCatalogValidationErrors, FromAPICreatePlanRequest, FromAPIUpsertPlanRequest, FromAPIBillingPlanPhase, FromAPIBillingRateCard, FromAPIBillingPrice, ToProRatingConfig.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DynamicPriceType and PackagePriceType are intentionally unsupported in v3 — return GenericConflictError, not a 500. Adding a new price type requires updating both ToAPIBillingPrice and FromAPIBillingPrice and the unsupportedV3PriceTypes map.

key_file_guides.role: Unit tests for all convert.go functions using table-driven subtests. Uses clock.SetTime for status tests, newTestPlan helper, and decimal/gobl/currency literals.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New conversion paths must have corresponding table-driven tests here. Use assert.Equal with typed API constants (api.BillingPlanStatus*, api.ISO8601Duration, api.Numeric) rather than plain strings.

key_file_guides.role: ListPlans with page-based pagination (default page 1, size 20). Calls hasUnsupportedV3Price to silently skip plans with dynamic/package prices.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The hasUnsupportedV3Price skip is temporary (FIXME comment). Do not add other silent-skip logic; return errors or expose the items.

key_file_guides.role: PublishPlan sets EffectiveFrom = clock.Now() in the decoder, not in the service layer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Using clock.Now() (not time.Now()) is required so tests can control the clock via clock.SetTime.

key_file_guides.role: ArchivePlan sets EffectiveTo = clock.Now() in the decoder.

anti_patterns: Using time.Now() instead of clock.Now() in decoder closures — breaks time-controlled tests.
Omitting req.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues = true in Create/Update decoders — rejects plans with non-critical validation issues.
Adding new price type support in ToAPIBillingPrice without also updating FromAPIBillingPrice, hasUnsupportedV3Price, and the round-trip tests in convert_test.go.
Omitting httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()) from any handler's options block.
Calling domain service methods from the encoder closure (third argument) — all service calls must happen in the operation closure (second argument).

decisions.decision: DynamicPriceType and PackagePriceType return GenericConflictError (409) in ToAPIBillingPrice
decisions.rationale: The v3 API does not yet have a wire format for these price types; returning a conflict error surfaces the limitation clearly to callers rather than silently dropping data.

decisions.decision: Labels round-trip via labels.ToMetadata / labels.FromMetadata, not direct map assignment
decisions.rationale: The labels package enforces key/value format rules and provides consistent nil-vs-empty handling across all handlers that touch label metadata.

decisions.decision: IgnoreNonCriticalIssues set in decoder, not in domain service
decisions.rationale: HTTP create/update callers expect lenient validation (non-critical issues allowed); the service layer retains the ability to surface those issues as warnings on the returned object without blocking persistence.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new lifecycle action (e.g. ClonePlan) following the established handler pattern with clock.Now() and nil-check
